summary: Cathy O'Neil celebrates the arrest of Norman Seabrook, head of the Correction Officers' Benevolent Association, who was charged with investing union members' pensions in a failing hedge fund for kickbacks. She details her long-standing disgust with Seabrook's fear-mongering radio ads that dehumanized Rikers prisoners while he systematically protected abusive guards. O'Neil documents his pattern of obstruction, including shutting down prison bus systems to prevent testimony against officers and training guards to abuse prisoners off-camera. She expresses hope that his removal will finally enable reform of Rikers Island.
key_points: Seabrook ran fear-mongering radio ads dehumanizing prisoners while protecting guards who beat handcuffed teenagers
He systematically obstructed reform by shutting down bus systems to block testimony and removing investigators he couldn't control
He trained officers to conduct abuse 'behind a curtain or off camera' and coached them on creating cover stories
The arrest charges involve investing union pension funds in a failing hedge fund for personal kickbacks
His removal may finally enable meaningful reform of Rikers Island's brutal conditions
money_quotes: We protect you from all the people who'd kill your wife and rape your daughter.
He's a bully who leads and protects an army of bullies.
This is a guy who, in order to prevent a prisoner from testifying against one of those officers, had the entire bus system from Riker's shut down.
This is a guy who told people to 'follow the rules' so that they don't have 'to sit down at a table and come up with a story that doesn't make any sense.'
I've honestly had nightmares about my sons somehow fucking up and going to Riker's, and I'd like those to end.
key_insight: Institutional corruption thrives when union leadership prioritizes protecting abusers over accountability, turning what should be oversight into systematic obstruction of justice.
